steps.title: Identify the angle correspondence under dilation
steps.content:
When a figure is dilated, all side lengths scale by the scale factor, but angles are preserved; they remain unchanged.

Triangle $ABC$ is dilated by scale factor $3$ to produce triangle $XYZ$, so corresponding vertices are: $A \leftrightarrow X$, $B \leftrightarrow Y$, and $C \leftrightarrow Z$.

Therefore, angle $X$ at vertex $X$ in the dilated triangle equals angle $A$ at the corresponding vertex in the original triangle.

steps.title: Read angle A from the diagram and conclude
steps.content: From the diagram, angle $A$ in triangle $ABC$ is labeled as $60^{\circ}$. Since dilation preserves angles, $\angle X=\angle A=60^{\circ}$. That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
